items.title: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
items.description: Provides quality generic medicines at much lower prices than branded drugs through dedicated outlets.
steps: Visit a PMBJP Kendra with a valid prescription.
Ask for the generic equivalent of the prescribed medicine.
Purchase medicines at 50% to 90% cheaper rates than market prices.
items.eligibility: Open to all citizens.
items.notes: Quality is certified by the Bureau of Pharma PSUs of India (BPPI).

items.title: Mission Indradhanush (Immunization)
items.description: Ensures full immunization for children up to 2 years and pregnant women against vaccine-preventable diseases.
steps: Check the immunization schedule at the local Anganwadi or Health Center.
Take the child/pregnant woman to the session site on the designated day.
Update the vaccination card after each dose.
items.eligibility: Children under 2 years and pregnant women.
items.notes: Covers 12 life-threatening diseases including TB, Polio, and Hepatitis B.

items.title: PM Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA)
items.description: Provides assured, comprehensive and quality antenatal care (ANC) free of cost on the 9th of every month.
steps: Visit a government health facility on the 9th of any month.
Undergo free diagnostic tests and physical examinations.
Receive specialized consultant advice for high-risk pregnancies.
items.eligibility: Pregnant women in their 2nd or 3rd trimester.
items.notes: Aims to reduce Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR).

items.title: Post-Matric Scholarship
items.description: Provides financial assistance to students from weaker sections studying at post-matriculation or post-secondary stages.
steps: Register on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Fill in academic details and bank information.
Submit the form for institutional verification.
items.eligibility: SC/ST/OBC students with family income within specified limits.
items.notes: Covers tuition fees and provides a maintenance allowance.

items.title: PM-Ushas (Higher Education Grant)
items.description: A central scheme to provide strategic funding to eligible state higher educational institutions.
steps: Institutions apply through the state higher education council.
Funds are disbursed based on performance and equity criteria.
items.eligibility: State Universities and Colleges.
items.notes: Focuses on improving quality and research in state institutions.

items.title: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMSS)
items.description: Scholarship for meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop out at class VIII.
steps: Sit for the selection test conducted by the State Government.
Qualify the examination with required percentage.
Apply for disbursement on NSP.
items.eligibility: Class 8 students with family income less than 3.5 Lakhs per annum.
items.notes: Amount is credited directly to the student's account annually.

items.title: Pragati Scholarship for Girls
items.description: Financial assistance for girls pursuing technical education (Degree or Diploma).
steps: Apply via the AICTE portal.
Upload admission proof and fee receipts.
Verification by the technical institute.
items.eligibility: Maximum two girls per family enrolled in AICTE approved courses.
items.notes: Provides 50,000 INR per year for every year of study.

items.title: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
items.description: Support for traditional artisans and craftspeople in skill upgradation and credit support.
steps: Register on the PM Vishwakarma portal using Aadhaar.
Undergo skill verification and basic training.
Receive toolkit incentive and collateral-free loans.
items.eligibility: Artisans working in 18 identified traditional trades (Carpenters, Potters, etc.).
items.notes: Provides a daily stipend during the training period.

items.title: PM-KUSUM (Solar Pump Jobs)
items.description: Provides subsidies to farmers to install solar water pumps and grid-connected solar power plants.
steps: Apply through the State Discom or Renewable Energy Department.
Site survey by officials.
Pay the farmer's share and get the system installed.
items.eligibility: Individual farmers, groups of farmers, or cooperatives.
items.notes: Excess power generated can be sold back to the government for income.

items.title: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
items.description: Placement-linked skill training program for rural poor youth.
steps: Register at the local PiIA (Project Implementation Agency).
Choose a trade for training.
Complete training and attend campus placements.
items.eligibility: Rural youth aged between 15 and 35 years.
items.notes: Guarantees placement for at least 70% of trained candidates.

items.title: Prime Minister’s Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
items.description: Credit linked subsidy scheme for setting up new micro-enterprises.
steps: Apply online on the KVIC website.
Submit project report and business plan.
Bank processes loan and government releases subsidy.
items.eligibility: Individuals above 18 years, at least 8th pass for large projects.
items.notes: Subsidy ranges from 15% to 35% of the project cost.

items.title: Tele-Law Service
items.description: Connects people to lawyers through video conferencing/telephone for pre-litigation advice.
steps: Visit the nearest Common Service Center (CSC).
Register your case with the Village Level Entrepreneur (VLE).
Speak to a lawyer via video call at the scheduled time.
items.eligibility: All citizens; free for those eligible under Section 12 of LSA Act.
items.notes: Available in multiple regional languages.

items.title: Nyaya Bandhu (Pro Bono Services)
items.description: A platform that connects verified pro bono advocates with eligible marginalized applicants.
steps: Register on the Nyaya Bandhu Android App or Portal.
Upload necessary documents for eligibility.
Wait for an advocate to accept your request.
items.eligibility: Poor, marginalized, or those in custody.
items.notes: Managed by the Department of Justice.

items.title: Lok Adalat
items.description: An alternative dispute redressal mechanism for fast and amicable settlement of pending cases.
steps: Apply to the court where the case is pending or to the DLSA.
Both parties must agree to settle via Lok Adalat.
Appear on the Lok Adalat date for a mediated settlement.
items.eligibility: Parties involved in compoundable civil or criminal disputes.
items.notes: The award by Lok Adalat is final and binding; no court fee is required.

items.title: Consumer Court Filing
items.description: File a complaint against unfair trade practices or defective goods/services.
steps: Send a formal notice to the seller/service provider.
File a complaint online via e-Daakhil or at the District Commission.
Pay the prescribed court fee (if applicable).
items.eligibility: Any consumer who has purchased goods or services.
items.notes: Cases up to 5 lakhs have no filing fee.

items.title: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
items.description: Direct income support of 6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families.
steps: Register on the PM-Kisan portal or through CSC.
Submit land ownership documents and Aadhaar.
Verification by the State Government.
items.eligibility: Small and marginal farmers with cultivable land.
items.notes: Payment is made in three equal installments of 2,000 every four months.

items.title: E-Shram Card
items.description: A national database of unorganized workers to facilitate social security benefits.
steps: Self-register on e-Shram portal or visit CSC.
Enter Aadhaar linked mobile number and OTP.
Fill in occupation and bank details to generate the card.
items.eligibility: Workers aged 16-59 years not covered under EPFO/ESIC.
items.notes: Includes free accidental insurance for one year.

items.title: Voter ID Registration
items.description: Register as a voter to participate in democratic elections.
steps: Fill Form 6 on the Voter Service Portal (Voters.eci.gov.in).
Upload photo and age/address proof.
Booth Level Officer (BLO) will visit for verification.
items.eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18 years and above.
items.notes: You can also apply for corrections using Form 8.

items.title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
items.description: Financial assistance to provide 'Housing for All' in rural and urban areas.
steps: Apply online through the PMAY portal or at a CSC.
Assessment by the local municipal or panchayat body.
Verification of land and current housing status.
documents_required: Affidavit of not owning a house
items.eligibility: Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) and Low Income Groups (LIG).
items.notes: Subsidies are provided on home loan interest or for house construction.

items.title: PAN Card Application
items.description: Essential identity for financial transactions and filing income tax returns.
steps: Apply online via NSDL or UTIITSL website.
Submit Aadhaar-based e-KYC for instant e-PAN.
Pay the processing fee for a physical card.
items.eligibility: All citizens, including minors and companies.
items.notes: Linking PAN with Aadhaar is mandatory for most users.

items.title: Income Certificate
items.description: Official document certifying the annual income of a person or family.
steps: Apply via the State e-District portal or Akshaya/CSC.
Submit salary slips or an income affidavit.
Village Officer conducts an inquiry and recommends approval.
items.eligibility: Any resident requiring proof of income for schemes.
items.notes: Validity usually ranges from 6 months to 1 year.

items.title: Caste/Community Certificate
items.description: Proof of belonging to a specific caste, required for reservation benefits.
steps: Submit application with proof of caste (parents' certificates).
Verification by the Revenue Department/Village Officer.
Collect the certificate from the Tehsildar office.
items.eligibility: Persons belonging to reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/EWS).
items.notes: OBC certificates often require a 'Non-Creamy Layer' status check.

items.title: Birth Certificate
items.description: The first legal document for an individual, recording date and place of birth.
steps: Register the birth at the hospital or local municipality within 21 days.
Apply for a copy at the Municipal/Panchayat office.
Download digitally signed copy from the state portal if available.
items.eligibility: Every child born in India.
items.notes: Late registration (after 21 days) requires an affidavit and police verification.

items.title: Driving License (Learner's)
items.description: Temporary permit to learn driving on public roads with an instructor.
steps: Apply online via the Parivahan Sarathi portal.
Upload ID and Address proof.
Take the online road signs test and pass to get the license.
items.eligibility: 16+ for gearless 2-wheelers; 18+ for geared vehicles.
items.notes: Valid for 6 months; permanent license can be applied for after 30 days.

items.title: Senior Citizen Bus Pass
items.description: Reduced fare or free travel for elderly citizens in state-run buses.
steps: Visit the RTC bus station or online portal.
Submit age proof and passport photos.
Collect the smart card or pass.
items.eligibility: Residents aged 60 years or above.
items.notes: Concession percentage varies by state (usually 25% to 50%).

items.title: Vehicle Registration (RC)
items.description: Legal registration of a motor vehicle with the government.
steps: Usually handled by the dealer during purchase.
Vehicle is inspected at the RTO.
RC is sent to the registered address by post.
items.eligibility: Owners of new or transferred motor vehicles.
items.notes: Registration is valid for 15 years for private vehicles.

items.title: High-Security Registration Plate (HSRP)
items.description: Mandatory tamper-proof number plates for all vehicles.
steps: Book an appointment on the official HSRP portal for your state.
Pay the fee online and choose a fitment center (Dealer).
Visit the dealer on the scheduled date to get the plate fixed.
items.eligibility: All vehicle owners.
items.notes: Mandatory by law to prevent vehicle theft and cloning.
